rulings.Case Title: BRUCE TALBERT ET AL VS. OWEN-ILLINOIS, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 7, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Resco Holdings L.L.C.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is Denied. Defendant failed to meet its burden of showing that Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence that Plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855). Defendant failed to address Steve Berg as a person with knowledge of Defendant's actions at Plaintiff's jobsites, identified in Plaintiff's responses to special interrogatory no. 8. (Kujawski Declaration, Ex. D at 29.) Plaintiff testified working in close proximity to Pullman employees working with gaskets, packing, and welding blankets, which created visible clouds of dust. (Deposition of Bruce Talbert at 282-283, 905-906.) Plaintiff's testimony that he took asbestos classes after his employment at Diablo Canyon in conjunction with his description of the products is circumstantial evidence that the products were asbestos-containing. (Id. at 933.) The sophisticated user defense applies to manufacturers and is therefore inapplicable to Defendant. (Johnson v. Am. Standard, Inc. (2008) 43 Cal. 4th 56, 61, 65.) The hearing will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/CML)

On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 7, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is off calendar. Withdrawal of motion filed by moving party on October 23, 2019. = (503/CML)
